pi_items.source: Verified as appropriate SQL Server functions for this task. DATEADD is standard SQL function for date arithmetic (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/t-sql/functions/dateadd-transact-sql). ISNULL/COALESCE are standard NULL handling functions.

Task description states 'fiscal year (2013)'. Export script filters Sales.SalesOrderDetail for orders with OrderDate >= '2013-01-01' AND < '2014-01-01' (line 63).

AdventureWorks2022 is an official Microsoft sample database for SQL Server, described as a sample for online transaction processing (OLTP). Verified from Microsoft Learn documentation (learn.microsoft.com).
AdventureWorks is a sample database for a fictitious company 'Adventure Works Cycles'. All product data, sales data, and customer data are synthetic examples designed for learning SQL.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the AdventureWorks2022 database, an official Microsoft sample OLTP database containing synthetic data for a fictitious manufacturing company. Product ID 707 is Sport-100 Helmet, Red (product number HL-U509-R, cost $13.09, list price $34.99). The task analyzes 2013 sales data (calendar year 2013) and uses 2014-01-01 as the anchor date for stockout projection calculations. The view should contain columns including ProductID, ProductNumber, ProductName, CurrentInventory (sum from Production.ProductInventory), TotalSales2013 (sum of OrderQty from Sales.SalesOrderDetail), AvgDailyBurnRate, EstimatedDaysSupply, and ProjectedStockoutDate. The stored procedure should filter results where EstimatedDaysSupply <= @RunwayDays parameter. The verifier performs spot checks on product 707 using direct SQL queries to compare calculated values against the view output.
